title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot reveals that Harriet Sipper, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nigges were already dead before 7:15 p.m. He explains that the three murders were part of a conspiracy. When the waiter, Raphael Burbank, delivered afternoon tea to Room 317 at 7:15 p.m., he believed the victims were alive because two accomplices were impersonating Harriet Sipper and Richard Nigges. The third person in the room, Edith Gransbury, was actually a corpse; she had been placed in a chair with her back to the door to hide the fact that she was dead and silent.

Poirot explains that the conspirators chose Room 317 instead of the more convenient Room 238 to avoid the risk of moving a corpse through the hotel corridors. To prevent suspicion regarding the uneaten food, the conspirators removed the tea and cakes from the scene. Poirot links the missing food to a homeless man seen earlier eating cake from a suitcase, deducing that the person impersonating Richard Nigges used a suitcase to transport the stolen food.

The impersonator attempted to create an alibi by discussing the bill with the receptionist, Thomas Brinell, to ensure Brinell would remember "Nigges" being alive at 7:30 p.m. However, the killer made a critical error: the impersonator claimed Nigges was wealthy enough to pay for everything, unaware that Nigges actually had little income or savings, a fact known to his brother, Henry Nigges.
